---
title: "Vignettes"
---

```{=html}
<style>
.vignette-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr);
  gap: 1rem;
  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
}
@media (max-width: 768px) {
  .vignette-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
}
.vignette-card {
  border: 1px solid #dee2e6;
  border-left: 4px solid #2780e3;
  border-radius: 6px;
  padding: 1.1rem 1.4rem;
  background: #fff;
  transition: box-shadow 0.15s;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.vignette-card:hover {
  box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.08);
}
.vignette-lang {
  font-size: 0.72rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.06em;
  color: #6c757d;
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
}
.vignette-title {
  font-size: 1rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
  line-height: 1.4;
}
.vignette-title a {
  color: #212529;
  text-decoration: none;
}
.vignette-title a:hover { color: #2780e3; }
.vignette-question {
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  color: #495057;
  font-style: italic;
  line-height: 1.5;
  flex: 1;
  margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
}
.vignette-packages {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.35rem;
  margin-top: auto;
}
.vignette-pkg {
  font-size: 0.75rem;
  font-family: monospace;
  background: #f1f3f5;
  color: #495057;
  border: 1px solid #dee2e6;
  border-radius: 3px;
  padding: 0.1rem 0.4rem;
}
</style>
```

These vignettes show how to work with IRW data for common psychometric tasks.

::: {.callout-note}
Many of the vignettes below — including the dataset search, compute scripts,
analysis design, and writing — were produced largely by Claude (Anthropic),
working from a task specification and with human review. Individual pages
note this explicitly where it applies; treat the methodological choices and
interpretations accordingly, and check the underlying compute scripts
directly if you're relying on the numbers shown.
:::

## R

Each of these vignettes uses the [`irw` R package](https://itemresponsewarehouse.github.io/Rpkg/) to fetch data and walks through a complete analysis.

<div class="vignette-grid">

<div class="vignette-card">
<div class="vignette-lang">R</div>
<div class="vignette-title"><a href="./imv.qmd">Calculating IMV Values</a></div>
<div class="vignette-question">How much does a 2PL improve on a 1PL for a given dataset?</div>
<div class="vignette-packages">
<span class="vignette-pkg">irw</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">mirt</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">imv</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="vignette-card">
<div class="vignette-lang">R</div>
<div class="vignette-title"><a href="./diffsim.qmd">Simulating Item Difficulties</a></div>
<div class="vignette-question">How can I simulate item difficulties that reflect real-world distributions rather than idealized assumptions?</div>
<div class="vignette-packages">
<span class="vignette-pkg">irw</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="vignette-card">
<div class="vignette-lang">R</div>
<div class="vignette-title"><a href="./2pl_across_datasets.qmd">2PL Parameters Across Datasets</a></div>
<div class="vignette-question">What do discrimination parameters look like across many cognitive/educational datasets in the wild?</div>
<div class="vignette-packages">
<span class="vignette-pkg">irw</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">mirt</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">ggplot2</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="vignette-card">
<div class="vignette-lang">R</div>
<div class="vignette-title"><a href="./cfa.qmd">Conducting CFA with IRW Data</a></div>
<div class="vignette-question">How do I fit a multi-factor CFA model to an IRW personality dataset?</div>
<div class="vignette-packages">
<span class="vignette-pkg">irw</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">lavaan</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">psych</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="vignette-card">
<div class="vignette-lang">R</div>
<div class="vignette-title"><a href="./item_text_difficulty.qmd">Item Text and Difficulty</a></div>
<div class="vignette-question">Can simple linguistic features of item wording predict proportion correct across cognitive datasets?</div>
<div class="vignette-packages">
<span class="vignette-pkg">irw</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">ggplot2</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="vignette-card">
<div class="vignette-lang">R</div>
<div class="vignette-title"><a href="./dutch_identity.qmd">The Dutch Identity</a></div>
<div class="vignette-question">What does Holland's (1990) Dutch Identity reveal about the structure of IRT models — and does it hold in real data?</div>
<div class="vignette-packages">
<span class="vignette-pkg">irw</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">mirt</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">imv</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">ggplot2</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="vignette-card">
<div class="vignette-lang">R</div>
<div class="vignette-title"><a href="./rt_imv.qmd">Response Time and Accuracy: An IMV Analysis</a></div>
<div class="vignette-question">How much does knowing how fast a person responded improve predictions of whether they answered correctly, over and above IRT ability estimates?</div>
<div class="vignette-packages">
<span class="vignette-pkg">irw</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">mirt</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">imv</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">splines</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="vignette-card">
<div class="vignette-lang">R</div>
<div class="vignette-title"><a href="./il_hte.qmd">Item-Level Heterogeneous Treatment Effects</a></div>
<div class="vignette-question">When do items in an RCT outcome measure respond differently to treatment, and what does ignoring this do to treatment-by-covariate interaction estimates?</div>
<div class="vignette-packages">
<span class="vignette-pkg">irw</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">lme4</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="vignette-card">
<div class="vignette-lang">R</div>
<div class="vignette-title"><a href="./hf_reliability_paradox.qmd">The Reliability Paradox in Cognitive-Control Tasks</a></div>
<div class="vignette-question">Do congruent/incongruent difference scores in IRW cognitive-control tasks show the same reliability collapse Hedge, Powell & Sumner (2018) found in the lab?</div>
<div class="vignette-packages">
<span class="vignette-pkg">irw</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">data.table</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">ggplot2</span>
</div>
</div>

</div>

## Python

These vignettes use the [`irw` Python package](https://github.com/itemresponsewarehouse/Python-pkg) to fetch data.

<div class="vignette-grid">

<div class="vignette-card">
<div class="vignette-lang">Python</div>
<div class="vignette-title"><a href="./irt_python.qmd">IRT Models in Python</a></div>
<div class="vignette-question">How do I fetch IRW data in Python and fit 1PL, 2PL, 3PL, and graded response models?</div>
<div class="vignette-packages">
<span class="vignette-pkg">irw</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">mirt</span>
<span class="vignette-pkg">girth</span>
</div>
</div>

</div>
